cross_connections.evidence: Thomas Edwin's father Thomas Gould (H01270) and Laura's father Myron (H01272) were half-brothers via Capt. Thomas Hooker (H00491) by different mothers. Two of Thomas Edwin's sons each married a Shader-line descendant of Laura: Thomas Harvey (H03872) m. Laura's daughter Caroline E. Shader (X02269); William Henry (H03880) m. Laura's granddaughter Lydia M. Robinson. Two cousin marriages closed the loop between the Thomas Gould and Myron lines.

Sandy Creek Cemetery, Murray, Orleans County NY (per Findagrave). Same as husband Thomas Edwin.

narrative_blocks.header: Married third cousin from Myron Hooker line
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker married Caroline E. 'Carrie' Shader on February 12, 1863. Both spouses were third cousins descending from Captain Thomas Hooker — Thomas Harvey through his father Thomas Edwin Hooker, and Caroline through her mother Laura Electa Hooker Shader whose father Myron Hooker was Thomas Edwin's half-brother. The cousin-marriage united two Hooker descent paths that had separated several generations earlier.

narrative_blocks.header: His cousin marriage merged two separated Hooker lines
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker's marriage to his third cousin Caroline Shader re-merged two Hooker family lines that had separated three generations earlier through the half-brother split between his father Thomas Edwin and her grandfather Myron. The cousin marriage was a deliberate or coincidental rejoin of two parallel Hooker descent streams in western New York. He and his wife maintained the Hooker surname throughout their marriage.

narrative_blocks.header: Eldest son of Thomas Edwin Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker was born July 15, 1840 in Murray, Orleans County New York — eldest son of Thomas Edwin Hooker and Lucy Hyde Hooker. The Hooker family had migrated to the western New York lake-plain region during the early nineteenth century along with many other Connecticut Yankees seeking new agricultural land.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Orleans County NY, died Stafford NY 1919
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker died March 21, 1919 in Stafford, Genesee County New York at age seventy-eight. His two children — Lucy and Alvah — both lived into the mid-twentieth century, with Alvah surviving until 1953 and Lucy until 1924.

burial.plot_notes: Stafford Rural Cemetery, Stafford Genesee County NY — same plot area as wife Caroline + daughter Alva + son-in-law Jean Mason + infant grandson H. Mason

birth.historical_notes: Per EH 3872: b. March 16, 1842 Wethersfield. WikiTree records "Wethersfield Springs, Wyoming County NY" — same location.

parents.mother_research_notes: Laura E. Hooker Shader (HD3260, b.1818 VT, Myron Hooker's daughter — making Caroline a Hooker descendant on her mother's side)

burial.plot_notes: Stafford Rural Cemetery, Stafford Genesee County NY — alongside husband Thomas Harvey and daughter Alva

bio.photo_notes: Photo shows David and Lucy together; same Cloudinary URL applied to both their entries

birth.historical_notes: Per EH 5030 + WikiTree: b. March 16, 1864 NY (WikiTree says "about 16 Mar 1864" matching EH).

death.note: Died Christmas Eve 1924 in Medina NY. Outlived first child Jay (KIA 1918) by six years. Sometimes recorded as "Louise" Hooker — informal alternate name (per Findagrave for daughter Etta E Hebner Cook).

marriages.notes: Per EH 3872: m. Jan 1, 1883 — New Year's Day wedding. WikiTree records David L. Hebner as her husband.

burial.plot_notes: Boxwood Cemetery, Medina Orleans County NY — same plot as husband David Conrad Hebner

bio.extra_names: EH 5031 spelling 'Alvah' with H — Findagrave + WikiTree spell 'Alva' (no H). Going with Findagrave/WikiTree spelling here since they're contemporary memorial sources.

birth.historical_notes: Per EH 5031: b. Feb 10, 1869 (WikiTree says "about 10 Feb 1869" matching EH).

death.note: d.1953 age ~83. Buried Stafford Rural Cemetery near her parents Thomas Harvey + Caroline Hooker, her husband Jean Mason Hooker (d.1914), and their infant child H. Mason Hooker (d.1906).

burial.plot_notes: Stafford Rural Cemetery, Stafford Genesee County NY — same plot area as parents Thomas Harvey + Caroline Shader Hooker, husband Jean Mason Hooker, and infant child H. Mason Hooker

death.note: Died Feb 16, 1940 at about age 93. WikiTree records age "about 93." Location not yet documented in available sources.

narrative_blocks.header: Born before the Mexican-American War; died in 1940
narrative_blocks.body: Emma Louisa Hooker Brockway was born September 3, 1846 in Murray, Orleans County, New York and died February 16, 1940 at approximately ninety-three — born two years before the Gold Rush and dying seven months after Germany invaded Poland. She married Isaac Wesley Brockway at Murray on March 30, 1879 and lived into a century that her parents, both born in the 1810s in the same upstate New York county, could not have imagined; her lifespan bridged the entire arc from the antebellum frontier to the eve of the Second World War.

narrative_blocks.header: Outlived husband Wesley by forty-five years
narrative_blocks.body: Her husband Wesley Brockway — formally Isaac Wesley Brockway, an Erie Canal coal merchant and boatman — died in 1895. Emma outlived him by forty-five years; she also outlived her only son Edwin by about a year, dying February 1940 at approximately ninety-three.

narrative_blocks.header: Inherited husband's three children from first marriage
narrative_blocks.body: When she married Wesley Brockway in March 1879 at thirty-two, he was forty-five and a widower with three children (Mabel, Archie, Fred) from his first marriage to Marion Peggs. Emma added one son Edwin in November 1880 — and would later see Edwin name his own first daughter Mabel for her older half-sister.

burial.plot_notes: Mount Albion Cemetery, Albion Orleans County NY

bio.extra_names: Known as 'Wesley' — middle name was his common-use name
bio.bio_blurb: Erie Canal boatman in the coal trade

birth.historical_notes: EH 3874 records birthplace as Murray NY but 1880 census records state of birth as New York generally; consistent.

parents.father_research_notes: Nathan Brockway, b. ~1806 New Hampshire (per 1855 Orleans NY Census, age 49). Recorded as farmer. Possibly distantly related to the Charles Merrill Brockway / Hiel Brockway line of Sweden Monroe NY (founders of Brockport on Erie Canal) — but parents are different (Nathan vs Charles Merrill); these would be at most distant cousins.
parents.mother_research_notes: Rachel (Thayer) Brockway, b. ~1806 Massachusetts (per 1855 Orleans NY Census, age 49)

careers.notes: Per EH 3874: 'He was a coal merchant.' Per 1880 US Census occupation: 'Boatman' — likely operated coal boats on the Erie Canal, which passed through the Murray-Hulburton area.

sources.page: Township: Murray, County: Orleans, State: New York, Series: T9, Roll: 1254912, Image: 616, Sheet: 198-A, Date: 1880, Line: 4. Occupation: Boatman.

narrative_blocks.header: An Erie Canal boatman in the coal trade
narrative_blocks.body: Isaac Brockway worked the Erie Canal through Orleans County as a boatman and coal merchant, the two trades complementary where coal moved by water. At forty-five he married Emma Hooker, his second wife, and died in 1895 — forty-five years before she did.

narrative_blocks.header: Coal and a canal boat at Hulburton
narrative_blocks.body: Both coal merchant and boatman where the Erie Canal ran through Orleans County, he carried the coal trade on the water. Widowed once, he married Emma Hooker at forty-five and was outlived by her forty-five years.

bio.extra_names: EH 3874 says b. Hulberton (variant spelling of Hulburton)

death.note: Per WikiTree: d.1941 about age 60. Specific date and location not documented.

sources.page: Entry 5032 (as Wesley + Emma Brockway child); listed simply as "Edwin H." with birth Nov 19, 1880 Hulberton
Township: Murray, County: Orleans, State: New York, Series: T624, Image: 959, Sheet: 11-A, Family: 204, Date: 1910, Line: 27. Wife Anna; children Mabel + Margery Anita.

burial.plot_notes: Sandy Creek Cemetery, Murray, Orleans County NY (per Findagrave). Same as husband Thomas Edwin and his first wife Lucy.

narrative_blocks.header: Married fellow Hooker descendant, third-cousin distance
narrative_blocks.body: William Henry 'Willie' Hooker married Lydia Mason Robinson — herself a Hooker descendant via her grandmother Laura E. Hooker Shader, daughter of Captain Thomas Hooker's son Myron Hooker. William Henry and Lydia were third cousins once removed; their marriage united two Hooker-line branches in a single household, with their son Charles Theodore Hooker holding documented double Hooker ancestry through both parents.

narrative_blocks.header: Two brothers each married a Shader-line Hooker cousin
narrative_blocks.body: The cousin marriage was paralleled in the family: William Henry's brother Thomas Harvey Hooker had also married a Shader-line Hooker cousin (Caroline E. Shader, Lydia's aunt). Two brothers Thomas Edwin Hooker's sons each thus married a Shader-line Hooker woman, making their offspring doubly Hooker-descended on both maternal and paternal sides. The pattern thickened the Hooker-line concentration in the Murray, Orleans County New York branch.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Murray NY 1864, three children with Lydia
narrative_blocks.body: William Henry Hooker was born August 18, 1864 in Murray, Orleans County New York — the eldest child of Thomas Edwin Hooker by his second wife Susan M. Brown. He died May 15, 1933 in Brockport, Monroe County New York at age sixty-eight, buried at Beach Ridge Cemetery Brockport. He had three children with Lydia: C. Theodore Hooker (1891-1971), Ethel Susan Hooker Zastrow (1896-1928 died young at 31), and Marjory Violetta Hooker Delahanty (1902-1993).

burial.plot_notes: Beach Ridge Cemetery, Brockport, Monroe County NY (per Findagrave). Same as wife Lydia Mason Robinson Hooker.

narrative_blocks.header: Cousin marriage united two Hooker-line branches
narrative_blocks.body: Lydia Mason Robinson married William Henry Hooker on January 19, 1889 — the marriage joining two Hooker-line branches in a single household. Lydia descended from Captain Thomas Hooker through her grandmother Laura Electa Hooker Shader (Myron Hooker's daughter); William Henry descended through the parallel Thomas Edwin Hooker branch. They were third cousins once removed, and their three children inherited double Hooker ancestry.

narrative_blocks.header: Three children, daughter Ethel died age thirty-one
narrative_blocks.body: Lydia and William Henry had three children: Charles Theodore Hooker (1891-1971), Ethel Susan Hooker Zastrow (1896-1928 died young at thirty-one), and Marjory Violetta Hooker Delahanty (1902-1993). The Hooker-Zastrow line traces forward through Ethel; the Hebner-Cook-Stafford family that descended from these children is doubly Hooker-descended on both maternal and paternal sides.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Murray NY 1865, died Holley 1915
narrative_blocks.body: Lydia Mason Robinson was born July 9 or 10, 1865 in Murray, Orleans County New York. She died February 8, 1915 in Holley, Orleans County New York at age forty-nine — predeceasing her husband William Henry by eighteen years. The Orleans County NY core of the Hooker-Shader-Robinson cousin network spanned three generations of intermarriages within the same upstate New York county.

burial.plot_notes: Beach Ridge Cemetery, Brockport, Monroe County NY (per Findagrave). GPS 43.1907564 -77.9415601.
Lakeview Cemetery, Brockport, Monroe County NY (per Findagrave). Section B Lot 35. GPS 43.1921568 -77.9418043.

narrative_blocks.header: Charles died young
narrative_blocks.body: Charles died in 1876, aged about nine.

narrative_blocks.header: Susanna died young
narrative_blocks.body: Susanna died in 1884 at the age of fifteen.

bio.title: Doctor
bio.bio_blurb: Frontier New York physician from Vermont

birth.historical_notes: Per EH 1270: b. Jan 5, 1781 Poultney VT. WikiTree says 1780; per Sam's rule EH overrides WikiTree on dates. Earlier database had erroneously listed Woodbury CT — that was wrong.

sources.page: Project completion contact for Chesterfield Hooker line research

narrative_blocks.header: A Vermont doctor who carried his trade west
narrative_blocks.body: Born in Poultney, Vermont, he trained as a physician and settled his family on the western New York frontier at Murray, where he practiced for decades. He and his wife died within eleven months of each other in 1856.

narrative_blocks.header: He buried four of his nine children young
narrative_blocks.body: Four of his nine children died before twenty-two — two boys at eight, and three more between seventeen and twenty-two — a punishing toll even by the standards of a frontier century.

burial.plot_notes: Sandy Creek Cemetery Murray Orleans County New York per FA
Sandy Creek Cemetery, Murray, Orleans County NY (per Findagrave). Same as husband Thomas Gould.
Congregational Cemetery Castleton Rutland County Vermont per FA

narrative_blocks.header: First two wives both died within two years
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Hooker married Ruth Parmelee in December 1779, and she died September 4, 1781 — less than two years later, shortly after bearing their son Thomas Gould; he then married Ruth Hickok in April 1783, and she died November 16, 1784, barely eighteen months after the wedding. His third wife, Mary 'Polly' Coleman, whom he married at Poultney, Vermont in January 1787, outlived him by twenty years, dying in 1858 at approximately ninety-six — she was the one who stayed.

military_service.notes: FA: officially registered as Captain and veteran of the Revolutionary War.

death.cause: died ~10 months after son's birth (likely childbirth complications)

parents.father_research_notes: Lieut. Jeniel Parmalee of Litchfield CT

burial.plot_notes: Hosford Crossing Cemetery Poultney Rutland County Vermont per FA
